Prism Interactive

Democratizing design-driven software for the enterprise

STAFF UX DESIGNER

Portland, OR (Hybrid - 2 days/week onsite) · Full-time · $240,000 - $310,000 per year

We're seeking an experienced Staff UX Designer to lead design excellence across multiple product verticals at
Prism Interactive. In this staff-level role, you'll drive technical design strategy, mentor junior designers, establish

design systems standards, and champion user-centered research methodologies across the organization. You'll
work across cross-functional teams including engineering, product, and customer success to ship impactful
features while maintaining design quality at scale. This is a leadership role for someone with 8-12+ years of

experience who thrives in technical environments and wants to shape design culture and practices organization-
wide.

ABOUT US

Prism Interactive is a rapidly growing SaaS company that provides enterprise software for design collaboration
and product management. Founded in 2018, we're headquartered in Portland, Oregon and serve over 2,000

companies globally. Our mission is to empower cross-functional teams to collaborate seamlessly on product
design and strategy, breaking down silos between design, engineering, and product management.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead and mentor a distributed team of 2-3 mid-level UX designers, providing constructive feedback on

designs, conducting design critiques, and fostering professional development

Drive the evolution of Prism's design system using Figma, establishing component libraries, design tokens,

and accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) that scale across multiple product areas

Conduct advanced user research including qualitative interviews, usability testing, and analysis workflows;

synthesize findings into actionable design recommendations for product roadmaps

Own the design and prototyping (using Figma prototyping and Framer) of cross-functional features
spanning multiple product surfaces, from conception through launch and iteration

Establish and document design system governance, versioning, and contribution guidelines; collaborate with
engineering on design-to-code handoff and component implementation

Champion accessibility compliance across all design deliverables; conduct WCAG audits, establish
accessibility testing protocols, and partner with QA on automated accessibility testing integration

Collaborate with technical leads and architects to evaluate and recommend design tools and technologies;
lead adoption and best practices for Figma plugins, design tokens, and automation

Represent design in product strategy meetings and roadmap planning; influence prioritization based on user

research insights and design technical feasibility

 Evangelize user-centered design practices across engineering and product teams through workshops, design
talks, and collaborative design sprints

REQUIRED

8-12+ years of professional UX/product design experience in SaaS, with demonstrated impact on shipped
products

Expert proficiency with Figma, including advanced features like design systems, variables, prototyping, and
API integration; proven ability to establish design system governance

Deep expertise in design system creation and component architecture; experience building scalable design
patterns and documentation

Strong background in user research methodologies (qualitative interviews, usability testing, workshop
facilitation) and the ability to synthesize insights into design direction

Advanced knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) and hands-on experience designing and testing
accessible interfaces for diverse users

Proven track record of technical leadership, mentoring junior designers, and driving design culture change
across multiple teams

Portfolio demonstrating 3+ substantial, shipped products with measurable user impact; case studies showing
design process, iteration, and outcomes

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with design tokens, component APIs, and design-to-code workflows (Storybook, Chromatic, or
similar)

Hands-on prototyping experience with Framer, Penpot, or similar advanced prototyping tools

Experience building or scaling design systems in fast-growing companies and managing cross-functional
adoption

Familiarity with user research tools and analytics (Maze, UserTesting, Hotjar, Amplitude) and ability to
interpret quantitative usage data

Background in enterprise software design and understanding of B2B SaaS workflows and complexity

TECH STACK

Figma (design systems, components, variables, prototyping, plugins)

  User research methodologies & analysis

WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards

  Design systems and component libraries

  Framer (advanced prototyping)

Design tokens and design-to-code workflows

  Accessibility testing tools (axe, WAVE)

  Figma API and plugin development

Qualitative research tools (Maze, UserTesting)

  Analytics and UX metrics (Amplitude, Hotjar)

BENEFITS

Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance (100% company-paid premiums for employees)

401(k) matching up to 6% with immediate vesting

 Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 20 days encouraged; 10 paid company holidays

Professional development budget of $3,500 annually for conferences, courses, and certifications

Remote work flexibility with 2 hybrid days/week in our Portland office

Design tools and equipment budget ($2,000 annually for monitors, peripherals, software)

Parental leave (12 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 6 weeks for secondary caregiver)

Mental health support through Headspace and access to an employee assistance program

HOW TO APPLY

Please submit your resume, a cover letter describing your experience with design systems and technical
leadership, and a portfolio link showcasing 2-3 of your most significant projects to careers@prisminteractive.com.
In your cover letter, highlight a specific example of how you've scaled design practices or mentored designers.
Submissions will be reviewed on a rolling basis. We're committed to building a diverse and inclusive design team
and encourage applications from underrepresented groups in design.
